channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Progress in ICT industry policy and cybersecurity standards drive MENA and Central Asia toward an intelligent era
item.description: Telecom networks are an integral backbone of the digital infrastructure that powers our economies today. They’re the driving force behind every industry’s digital transformation, connecting businesses, people, and information like never before.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Handle demanding LLMs and large-scale AI inferencing with purpose-built servers
item.description: Generative AI (genAI) has created much hype and excitement for enterprises with its promises of new possibilities, from process automation and content creation to improved customer service and enhanced productivity.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Redbox owner’s bankruptcy filing is the final nail in the coffin for DVDs
item.description: Those red DVD kiosks peppering Walgreens across the country may have reached their end. Last week, media company Chicken Soup for the Soul Entertainment, which owns Redbox, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: California considers unique safety regulations for AI companies, but faces tech firm opposition
item.description: California lawmakers are weighing a bill that would regulate powerful artificial intelligence systems.
item.dc:creator: Trân Nguyễn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Philips Hue's new powerful smart lights can illuminate every nook and cranny in your home
item.description: Housing powerful bulbs, each of the six Pillar and Runner spotlights emit bright light and can be controlled via Alexa.
item.dc:creator: cesartechradar@gmail.com (Cesar Cadenas) , TechRadar

item.title: Apple declares the iPhone X and original AirPods ‘vintage’—and they’re on the road to ‘obsolete’
item.description: One of the most iconic iPhone models is entering a new phase of life as Apple takes another step toward sunsetting it forever. The company has reclassified the iPhone X, along with the original AirPods and HomePod, as “vintage” technology.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: EU says Meta’s paid ad-free option breaks its digital competition rules
item.description: European Union regulators accused social media company Meta Platforms on Monday of breaching the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch 7: Revolutionary Health Alerts & Family Health Data Sharing
item.description: The upcoming Samsung Galaxy Watch 7 is set to feature groundbreaking health alerts, warning users of potential heart attacks, strokes, and more, alongside a new family health data sharing capability.
item.dc:creator: cesartechradar@gmail.com (Cesar Cadenas) , TechRadar

item.title: Eclipse Theia IDE arrives for desktop, browser
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has announced the release of the Eclipse Theia IDE. Based on the Theia cloud and desktop IDE framework implemented in TypeScript, the Theia IDE is avialable for Windows, Linux, and macOS and can be tried online.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Protect Yourself from Advanced Voice Scams: Top 10 Threats and Response Strategies
item.description: Stay ahead of sophisticated voice scams by understanding the latest tactics used by fraudsters. Learn how to identify and prevent these threats, and take proactive steps to safeguard your finances.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Evolving hybrid cloud strategies in modern agencies
item.description: How are the CDC and TSA managing cloud adoption to meet their missions? The post Evolving hybrid cloud strategies in modern agencies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: EU finds Meta's 'pay or consent' advertising model violates tech rules
item.description: The European Union said Meta’s “pay or consent” advertising model for European users violates the bloc’s Digital Markets Act (DMA), according to preliminary findings released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The technology powering Taylor Swift, Netflix and the Sphere
item.description: Disguise is a pioneer in an exploding market for visual graphics in demand from Hollywood to the NBA.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Nutanix hunts disgruntled VMware customers
item.description: The new Nutanix is virtually unrecognizable from the company that created the hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) market in 2011.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Supreme Court keeps on hold efforts in Texas and Florida to regulate social media platforms
item.description: The Florida and Texas laws were signed by Republican governors in the months following decisions by Facebook and Twitter, now X, to cut then-President Donald Trump off over his posts related to the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol by his supporters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The need for Supreme Court review grew more dire when federal appeals courts reached opposite conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday wiped lower court rulings regarding two state laws aimed at barring social media companies from banning users based on their political views, kicking the major First Amendment challenge down the road.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: DoD’s Joint Staff GenAI sprint lays out 8 internal use cases
item.description: Lt. Gen. Todd Isaacson, the CIO for the Joint Chiefs of Staff/J-6, said a review team is completing recommendations for leadership around using GenAI. The post DoD’s Joint Staff GenAI sprint lays out 8 internal use cases first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How to trace and block anonymous calls
item.description: Do you want to prevent scam calls? Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how to block hidden IDs on your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alibaba's Data Center Operations in India & Australia to be Shut Down
item.description: In a strategic move, Alibaba Cloud, the cloud computing arm of e-commerce giant Alibaba Group, has announced plans to cease its data center operations in India and Australia, as part of efforts to optimize its global footprint and enhance overall efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Boeing acquiring supplier Spirit AeroSystems for $4.7B
item.description: Boeing said Monday that it plans to purchase Spirit AeroSystems in a $4.7 billion all-stock deal decades after it spun off the supplier. The planemaker has for years outsourced parts of its production process to the Spirit.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Uncovering Ancient Secrets: Fossilized Dung Reveals Arizona Museum's Prehistoric Tales
item.description: Discover how an Arizona museum showcases the fascinating stories of prehistoric animals through their fossilized feces, offering a unique glimpse into ancient ecosystems and the creatures that inhabited them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EU Accuses Meta of Violating Digital Rules with Paid Ad-Free Option
item.description: The European Union has accused social media giant Meta Platforms of breaching its digital competition rulebook by requiring Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying a fee to avoid them, sparking concerns over the impact on user privacy and online advertising.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Revolutionizing Waterway Cleanup: Autonomous Robot Boat Eliminates Waste
item.description: Discover how the Class 3 Clearbot, an innovative autonomous vessel, is revolutionizing marine trash removal with its efficient and sustainable solution
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why (and perhaps how) Apple Intelligence will make money
item.description: We’ll leave it to future retrospectives to show us if it turns out be a good or a bad thing, but artificial intelligence will change everything, so of course Apple is building a business around it. That shouldn’t surprise anyone.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft Continues Enhancing Windows 11 Insider Previews with Latest Build
item.description: As Microsoft rolls out the Windows 11 2023 Update, it's also refining its latest preview builds to ensure a seamless user experience
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: InfoWorld Technology of the Year Awards 2024 Nominations Now Open
item.description: The 2024 InfoWorld Technology of the Year Awards will recognize the best and most innovative products across 26 categories in software development, cloud computing, data analytics, and AI and machine learning. Nominations for the 2024 awards are now open.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Unlock Data-Driven Insights with These 7 Essential Analytics Steps
item.description: Discover how to empower your business with data-driven decision-making by leveraging analytics, predictive models, AI capabilities, and visualizations, driving meaningful results for your organization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Instagram creators could soon make AI versions of themselves
item.description: Think you've seen them? Meta said “AI” will appear in front of the creator’s name followed by a “beta” tag after.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Will China try to make a land grab on the moon?
item.description: “A lot of their so-called civilian space program is a military program. And I think, in effect, we are in a race,” says NASA Administrator Bill Nelson.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Animal rescuers try to keep dozens of dolphins away from Cape Cod shallows after mass stranding
item.description: Animal rescuers were trying to keep dozens of dolphins away from shallow waters around Cape Cod after 125 of the creatures earlier stranded themselves.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Supreme Court nears the end of another momentous term. A decision on Trump's immunity looms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the last 10 days of June, on a frenetic pace of its own making, the Supreme Court touched a wide swath of American society in a torrent of decisions on abortion, guns, the environment, health, the opioid crisis, securities fraud and homelessness.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As AI gains a workplace foothold, states are trying to make sure workers don't get left behind
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — With many jobs expected to eventually rely on generative artificial intelligence, states are trying to help workers beef up their tech skills before they become outdated and get outfoxed by machines that are becoming increasingly smarter.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon is reviewing whether Perplexity AI improperly scraped online content
item.description: Amazon is reviewing claims that the artificial intelligence startup Perplexity AI is scraping content — including from prominent news sites — without approval.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration
item.description: How will healthcare continue to transform? The post Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for Biden administration in a social media dispute with conservative states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday sided with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states over how far the federal government can go to combat controversial social media posts on topics including COVID-19 and election security.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Stability AI Reboot: New CEO & Investor Group to Revitalize AI Image-Generator Startup
item.description: London-based Stability AI, the company behind the popular AI-powered image generator Stable Diffusion, is undergoing a major revamp with the appointment of a new CEO and a significant investment injection from a group that includes Sean Parker, co-founder of Napster and former president of Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
